constraints: generated lazy WebAudio music and SFX

framework_decision.reason: The current repository already ships a static single-file canvas game through frontend/index.html; the design keeps the static Vercel target reproducible while documenting the Phaser production path for future modular rebuilds.

visual_target.quality_bar: layered parallax, readable HUD, deterministic decoration, no protected assets, no per-frame shimmer for stable world art
forbidden: runtime watsonx credentials in the deployed game

acceptance.verify: npm run verify passes and checks design/export/static markers
acceptance.smoke: npm run smoke passes where Playwright browsers are installed
acceptance.dpr: DPR 1 and DPR 2 debug state remains valid
acceptance.mobile: touch-sized viewport starts and exposes mobile controls
acceptance.progression: final gate cannot skip Rogue Architect

evidence_policy: Do not claim production-ready without current build, verify, smoke, and deployment evidence.
Do not invent Matrix commit IDs or public URL status.
Keep secrets out of the repo; build.sh may read .env but never prints API keys.

batch_roadmap.title: Contract reset and scaffold
batch_roadmap.acceptance: npm run build and npm run verify pass

batch_roadmap.title: Framework foundation and viewport invariant
batch_roadmap.acceptance: DPR 1 and DPR 2 debug state is sane

batch_roadmap.title: Art direction and deterministic asset pipeline
Hero movement, physics, death, and respawn
batch_roadmap.acceptance: jump edge and pit/respawn markers exist

batch_roadmap.title: Rewards, panels, HUD, and Matrix Gates
Enemies and power-ups
batch_roadmap.acceptance: central damage and power-up behavior remains testable

batch_roadmap.title: Rogue Architect boss and final validation
batch_roadmap.acceptance: final gate is locked while bossAlive is true

batch_roadmap.title: WebAudio music, SFX, and atmosphere
batch_roadmap.acceptance: audio initializes only after user gesture

batch_roadmap.title: Mobile controls, accessibility, and UX polish
QA, evidence, tutorial, and release package

repair_policy.D: Design repair when mdesign validation or export fails.
repair_policy.B: Viewport/layout repair for DPR, clipping, or mobile issues.
repair_policy.C: Gameplay/audio progression repair for softlocks, boss skips, respawn failures, or audio lifecycle bugs.
